title: Cloud Computing
description: Introduction to cloud computing architectures, services, and deployment models. Covers IaaS, PaaS, SaaS, virtualization, containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), serverless computing, cloud storage systems, and distributed computing patterns. Students gain hands-on experience with major cloud platforms (AWS/Azure/GCP) through lab exercises deploying and managing cloud applications. Topics include auto-scaling, load balancing, microservices architecture, and cloud security. The prerequisite CS201 provides data structures knowledge needed for understanding distributed data storage and processing systems. This course is typically offered in Spring semester.
topics: Cloud service models: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
outcomes: Design and deploy applications on cloud platforms
Evaluate cost and performance of cloud architectures
